definitions.text: To finish; to make done; to reach the end.
examples: He completed the assignment on time.
The last chapter completes the book nicely.

definitions.text: To call from the small blind in an unraised pot.
With all parts included; with nothing missing; full.
examples: My life will be complete once I buy this new television.
She offered me complete control of the project.
When your homework is complete, you can go and play with Martin.
It was a complete shock when he turned up on my doorstep.
